mission.title: Install and Configure Logging Operator Kube Logging- on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Logging Operator automates the deployment and configuration of a Kubernetes logging pipeline, utilizing Fluent Bit for log collection and forwarding. Installing this operator simplifies the management of logs in your Kubernetes environment, ensuring secure and efficient log handling.

steps.title: Install the Logging Operator via Helm
steps.description:
Add the kube-logging Helm repository and install:
```bash
helm repo add kube-logging https://kube-logging.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
kubectl create namespace logging
helm install logging-operator kube-logging/logging-operator --namespace logging --version 4.11.0
```

steps.title: Create a Logging resource
steps.description:
Deploy a Logging pipeline with Fluent Bit and Fluentd:
```bash
kubectl apply -n logging -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: logging.banzaicloud.io/v1beta1
kind: Logging
metadata:
  name: default-logging
spec:
  fluentd: {}
  fluentbit: {}
  controlNamespace: logging
EOF
```

steps.title: Create an Output and Flow
steps.description:
Route logs to stdout (for testing):
```bash
kubectl apply -n logging -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: logging.banzaicloud.io/v1beta1
kind: ClusterOutput
metadata:
  name: stdout
spec:
  stdout: {}
---
apiVersion: logging.banzaicloud.io/v1beta1
kind: ClusterFlow
metadata:
  name: all-logs
spec:
  globalOutputRefs:
    - stdout
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n logging
kubectl get logging -n logging
kubectl get clusterflow -n logging
kubectl get clusteroutput -n logging
```

resolution.summary: Logging Operator is installed with Fluent Bit and Fluentd, collecting and routing logs from all pods.

uninstall.title: Remove flows and outputs
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl delete clusterflow --all -n logging
kubectl delete clusteroutput --all -n logging
kubectl delete logging --all -n logging
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Helm release
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall logging-operator -n logging
kubectl delete namespace logging
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade via Helm
upgrade.description:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade logging-operator kube-logging/logging-operator --namespace logging
```

troubleshooting.title: Fluent Bit pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
kubectl get ds -n logging
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentbit -n logging
```

troubleshooting.title: Logs not reaching the output
troubleshooting.description:
Check the Fluentd logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd -n logging
```

troubleshooting.title: CRD version mismatch
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure CRDs are up to date:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep logging
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl configured to interact with it.
